title: Understanding Your PF
scenario: Reading your payslip, you notice a chunk of salary goes to your Provident Fund each month, with your employer adding a matching share. HR mentions you can make a voluntary top-up to grow this tax-advantaged retirement corpus faster. You have ₹3,000 of monthly room in your budget.

choices.label: Direct ₹3,000/month extra into your PF as a voluntary contribution
choices.lesson: PF earns steady tax-advantaged interest and the employer match is effectively free money. Topping up young gives decades of compounding.

choices.label: Treat PF as 'locked-away money' and ignore the top-up option
choices.lesson: PF feels invisible because it is automatic, but it is a powerful long-horizon asset. Understanding your payslip helps you use benefits you already pay for.
